interactions.summary: Jiang concludes the game-theory layer: Iran, Israel, and Donald Trump all want America to use ground troops in Iran for selfish reasons, and all three may get what they want. He then says game theory alone cannot explain the war because the players also understand it through prophecy and eschatology.

He closes by acknowledging viewer concerns about his public exposure and family, saying he was a high school teacher who became internet famous overnight. He promises to keep explaining the war while becoming more careful about his profile and family safety.

speaker_notes.note: Continuous single-speaker closing monologue; questions in seg-0017 are Jiang's rhetorical prompts.

claims.claim: Jiang predicts that Iran, Israel, and Donald Trump all want America to use ground troops in Iran, and that this convergence is why ground troops will eventually be used.
His predicted upshot is that Israel removes America from the Middle East and becomes the Empire of Israel, Iran unites the Muslim world behind its leadership, and Trump probably gets a third term.
Jiang says game theory is not enough to explain the Middle East war because the major players also understand it as the fulfillment of biblical prophecy and as an eschatological event.
claims.temporal_scope: Interpretive transition made on 2025-06-22, previewing the next video.

claims.claim: Jiang frames his new public role as an educator's mission: he wants to keep explaining how the war may unfold so viewers are better prepared, even while limiting public access to protect his family.
claims.temporal_scope: Personal mission statement and public-profile adjustment on 2025-06-22.

signature_moments.moment: Three enemies converge on one desire: Iran, Israel, and Trump all want American ground troops in Iran.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Donald Trump, Israel, and Iran, want America to use ground troops in Iran
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is the synthesis of the entire game-theory pass and the reason Jiang thinks the bad move happens anyway.

signature_moments.moment: Game theory reaches its limit when the players think they are acting inside the end of the world.
signature_moments.source_phrase: using game theory itself, it's not going to work
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It preserves the transition from strategic calculation to religious obsession, which is the bridge to the next episode.

signature_moments.moment: The analyst is also suddenly a public figure: a high school teacher becomes internet famous and must decide how to continue without exposing his family.
signature_moments.source_phrase: I was a high school teacher. I was teaching my students. And now I'm Internet famous.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives the episode a human close and explains why the predictive-history role becomes an explicit mission.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang defines it as the understanding of how the world ends and says the Iran war is being read through that frame by major players.
Jiang's predicted Israeli regional-hegemonic outcome after America is removed from the Middle East.
Jiang's stated educator role: studying the war and explaining how it may unfold so viewers are better prepared.

chronology_notes.note: This source explicitly tees up the next video as a religious/eschatological explanation of the same Iran-war frame.
Jiang marks a shift in public posture on 2025-06-22: after sudden attention, he says he will continue making videos but protect his family and reduce public exposure.
